Contributor.Organization: The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

Channel.Extract-Protocol: Subcutaneous adipose needle biopsies were obtained from near the umbilicus of men from the METSIM cohort. RNA was isolated using the QIAGEN miRNeasy kit. Libraries were constructed using a polyA+ selection protocol (Illumina TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit v2)

Sample.Data-Processing: Fastx toolkit was used to remove low quality reads; reads with phred quality > 20 for 80% of bases were retained. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

Sample.Data-Processing: Fastx toolkit was used to remove low quality reads; reads with phred quality > 20 for 80% of bases were retained. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

Sample.Data-Processing: Fastx toolkit was used to remove low quality reads; reads with phred quality > 20 for 80% of bases were retained. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

Sample.Data-Processing: Fastx toolkit was used to remove low quality reads; reads with phred quality > 20 for 80% of bases were retained. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

STAR (version 2.4.2a) was used as the aligner, with hg19 and GENCODE v19 as annotation; STAR was run, using a 2-pass strategy, were splice locations from the first pass were used as additional annotation for the second pass. salmon (version 0.7.2) was used to estimate isoform abundance from aligned BAM files; GENCODE 19 was used as annotation. --gcBias option was used. tximport (version 1.0.3) was used to collapse isoform abundance estimates from salmon to gene abuddance estimates Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Abundance measurements generated using salmon and tximport; values represent the transcripts per million (TPM) estimate of gene abundance.

Sample.Data-Processing: Fastx toolkit was used to remove low quality reads; reads with phred quality > 20 for 80% of bases were retained. Tagdust was used to identify and remove reads containing adapter and linker sequences. Series.Title: Subcutaneous adipose gene expression from men in the METSIM cohort
Series.Summary: We analyzed the abdominal subcutaneous adipose tissue of 434 male human subjects from the METSIM study, via RNA-sequencing. The METSIM study was approved by the Ethics Committee of the University of Eastern Finland and Kuopio University Hospital in Kuopio, Finland and carried out in accordance with the Helsinki Declaration. Written informed consent was obtained from all participants. Here, we provide the expression values by gene for each subject and relevant covariates. Raw fastq files will be contributed to dbGaP, as they require controlled access.
Series.Overall-Design: Subcutaneous adipose gene expression profiles, measured via RNAseq, for 434 men from the METSIM cohort
Series.Type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
